sections.paragraph:
παρεμπλέκω (Diphilus, physician [III b.c.] in Athen. 2, 49, 57c; Hero Alex. I p. 20, 11; Oribasius, Ecl. 40: CMG VI 2, 2 p. 202, 7 π. τῷ ποτῷ τὴν τροφήν; schol. on Pind., Eustath., Prooem. 9; PTurin 8, 28 [116 b.c.]) med. t.t. mix, mingle, blend with, in ext. sense of false teachers ἑαυτοῖς παρεμπλέκουσιν Ἰησοῦν Χριστόν they mingle Jesus Christ with themselves (=their teaching) ITr 6:2 (s. the vv.ll. in Bihlmeyer’s app.).—DELG s.v. πλέκω.

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

Oribasius Oribasius , IV a.d.—List 5

CMG CMG = Corpus Medicorum Graecorum—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Eustath Eustath , XII a.d.—List 5

PTurin PTurin = APeyron, Papyri graecae . . . Taurinensis—List 4

med. med. = medicus (medical writer), medical

t.t. t.t. = terminus technicus (termini technici), technical term(s)

ext. ext. = extension

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

vv.ll. vv.ll. = variae lectiones (variant readings)

Bihlmeyer Bihlmeyer = KB., Die Apostolischen Väter—List 6

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 775
